title: senna artemisioides subsp. helmsii
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. helmsii, also known as Helms' senna or parry senna, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the legume family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative and in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. The saponins and anthraquinones present in Senna artemisioides subsp. helmsii contribute to its health benefits, which include the relief of constipation and other digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a herbal tea or used in combination with other herbs for its laxative properties, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
